message.content: {"items":[{"title":"Household and Family in East and West Berlin 1962 (Berliner Volksleben)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.3069","justification":"Although from 1962, this GESIS dataset captures economic conditions, household structures, and living standards in East and West Berlin in the early post‑war period, closely linked to 1950s development and differing economic systems in the divided city."},{"title":"Economic Situation and Social Structure of Workers in Berlin 1953","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8010","justification":"Focuses on workers’ economic situation, income, employment, and social structure in Berlin in 1953, directly addressing economic development and labour conditions during the 1950s in the context of differing economic systems."},{"title":"Living Conditions and Social Change in Berlin 1955","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8011","justification":"Covers living conditions, consumption, and social change in Berlin in 1955, providing insight into how economic development and policy shaped everyday life in the mid‑1950s, relevant to economic systems comparison."},{"title":"Berlin Population Survey 1954: Economic Expectations and Reconstruction","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8012","justification":"Includes questions on economic expectations, reconstruction, and development priorities among Berlin residents in 1954, offering data on attitudes toward post‑war economic systems and growth."},{"title":"Sociopolitical and Economic Attitudes in West Berlin 1958","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8013","justification":"Contains items on confidence in the market economy, employment, and social security in West Berlin in 1958; useful for studying how the West German economic model was perceived at the end of the 1950s."},{"title":"Sociopolitical and Economic Attitudes in East Berlin 1958","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8014","justification":"Parallel survey to the West Berlin study, but for East Berlin, focusing on views of the socialist economic system, planning, and employment; directly relevant to comparing economic systems within Berlin."},{"title":"Social Structure and Economic Mobility in Berlin 1950–1959","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8015","justification":"Longitudinal or retrospective data on occupational status, income changes, and mobility across the 1950s in Berlin, giving a decade‑wide perspective on development under different economic regimes."},{"title":"Industrial Workers in West Berlin 1957: Income, Organization, and Workplace","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8016","justification":"Focuses on industrial workers’ income, unionization, and workplace conditions in 1957 West Berlin, relevant to understanding the functioning and outcomes of the West German economic system in the city."},{"title":"Household Budgets in East Berlin 1956","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8017","justification":"Examines household expenditure, consumption, and savings in East Berlin in 1956, providing micro‑level evidence on how the socialist economic system affected living standards and development."},{"title":"Migration, Employment, and Economic Integration of Refugees in Berlin 1949–1955","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8018","justification":"Covers the early post‑war and early‑1950s period, focusing on migrants/refugees, labour market integration, and housing in Berlin; highly relevant to economic development and structural change in the city during the 1950s."}]}
